newsSinceJun6: Stock fell -14.4% today (now ~30% off the June 3 record high) on flow, not thesis breakage: wireless-unit head Harish Krishnaswamy liquidated his entire 1.39M-share stake (~71 SEK) and a Swedish institution also fully exited; FY2025 net loss was restated wider to 222.6 MSEK (from 186.5 MSEK) under PCAOB audit standards ahead of the June 15 AGM/Nasdaq listing vote. Counterintuitively, the actual fresh fundamental news today was positive: an $8.2M ALL.SPACE production order for Ka-band beamforming ICs (2027 delivery) - though that is SATCOM/defense, not the CPO laser thesis. The June 2 GlobalFoundries silicon-photonics/SCALE laser-array partnership (the CPO thesis anchor) remains intact; Q1 project pipeline was reported +77% to $799M.

sources.label: Ad-hoc-news: Executive and fund offload entire stakes as stock retreats from record high
Ad-hoc-news: 77% pipeline surge meets revised 222.6 MSEK loss ahead of Nasdaq vote
PR Newswire: ALL.SPACE awards $8.2M production order to Sivers (Jun 9, 2026)
Semiconductor Today: Sivers and GlobalFoundries silicon photonics for AI infrastructure (Jun 2, 2026)
Sivers IR: directed share issue ~125 MSEK (Apr 16, 2026 - pre-existing, not new)

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already cut the name for post-rerating crowding; today's -14.4% is flow-driven (insider/fund full exits, restated loss, pre-AGM nerves) with the GF CPO laser thesis intact, which partially restores asymmetry and offsets the fresh governance flags - no second consecutive down is warranted, but the insider exit and PCAOB restatement justify a small negative drift.
verdicts.bull: The GlobalFoundries SCALE/CPO laser-array design-in is unchanged and a $8.2M ALL.SPACE production order landed today; a 30% drawdown from the June 3 peak on flow rather than fundamentals re-opens some entry asymmetry into the June 15 Nasdaq listing vote.
verdicts.neutral: After +1687% YTD the name is no longer hidden at $2.46B mcap; the sell-off de-crowds it somewhat but most of the residual alpha this list rewards has already been harvested, and the CPO revenue is still pre-commercial reference-design stage.
verdicts.bear: A division head liquidating his entire 1.39M-share stake plus a restated FY25 net loss (222.6 MSEK) under PCAOB scrutiny ahead of a US listing are classic late-cycle distribution signals; if the Nasdaq vote or further insider filings sour sentiment, a +1687% YTD chart has a long way to fall.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: June 15 AGM/Nasdaq listing vote and first commercial design wins flowing from the GlobalFoundries SCALE/CPO laser-array partnership.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Continued insider/institutional distribution and governance overhang (PCAOB restatement) deflating a parabolic +1687% YTD rerating before CPO revenue materializes.
